assertions.reason: README.md matches the consolidated canonical deployment narrative with current network config, canonical contract addresses, and live proof transaction links.
evidence: README.md Live Network table lists chain ID 420420417, ETH RPC https://eth-rpc-testnet.polkadot.io/, fallback RPC https://services.polkadothub-rpc.com/testnet/, explorer, and faucet.

README.md Canonical Deployment tables include manifest addresses such as AccessManager 0x32d0a9eb8F4Bd54F0610c31c277fD2E62e4ac2f0, PvmQuoteProbe 0x9a78F65b00E0AeD0830063eD0ea66a0B5d8876DE, and LendingCore 0x9faC289188229f40aBfaa4F8d720C14b8B448CF9.

Read-only Python check confirmed README contains all contract addresses from dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical.json and the borrow/repay/liquidation proof tx hashes from dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical-results.json.
README.md Live Proof TX Links section includes 0x5a9edd08..., 0x02825742..., 0xeec68ce0..., 0x282f3253..., 0x4f55eac1..., 0xc147ac14..., and governance proof links.

assertions.reason: dualvm/SPEC.md describes the PVM quote engine as live product-path interop and contains no parity-only/frozen wording.
evidence: dualvm/SPEC.md states: 'The product-path LendingCore calls RiskAdapter → PvmQuoteProbe for every risk quote. This is live cross-VM interop — the PVM engine actively serves the product path.'
dualvm/SPEC.md documents the deployed PvmQuoteProbe code hash 0xba8fe2a621062a30bba558a3846d0a18bfb2e9a09bfaed656b123e698b59af5b and current network chain ID 420420417.
Grep on dualvm/SPEC.md for 'parity|frozen' returned no matches.

assertions.reason: dualvm/features.json currently describes the PVM posture as live product-path interop rather than frozen parity-only wording.
evidence: dualvm/features.json contains feature id 'pvm-interop' with title 'Live PVM interop: PvmQuoteProbe serves product-path risk quotes via RiskAdapter'.
The same entry points to current evidence artifacts: deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-probe-results.json, deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-probes.json, and ../docs/dualvm/dualvm_vm_interop_proof.md.
Read-only Python inspection found the only PVM feature title is the live-interop wording above.

assertions.reason: STATUS.md exists at repo root and contains the expected current network configuration including chain ID 420420417.
evidence: STATUS.md header reads 'Network: Polkadot Hub TestNet · Chain ID 420420417'.
STATUS.md contract table includes canonical addresses such as AccessManager 0x32d0a9eb8F4Bd54F0610c31c277fD2E62e4ac2f0 and LendingCore 0x9faC289188229f40aBfaa4F8d720C14b8B448CF9.
STATUS.md also includes live proof links for borrow, repay, liquidation, PVM echo, PVM quote, migration, and XCM weighMessage.

assertions.reason: The docs/dualvm tree does not contain the audited stale contradiction phrases, and the current docs explicitly describe live PVM interop using current proof artifacts and addresses.
evidence: Glob of /home/kpa/polkadot/docs/dualvm found three narrative markdown docs (dualvm_submission_final.md, dualvm_vm_interop_proof.md, dualvm_migration_format_proof.md) plus evidence assets; no older dualvm spec variants remained in that tree.
Grep of /home/kpa/polkadot/docs/dualvm for 'not proven live|parity-only|frozen at parity|frozen parity|fallback module' returned no matches.
Grep of /home/kpa/polkadot/docs/dualvm for 'baseline|versioned|governed manifest|superseded|old manifest|previous deployment|current deployment|canonical deployment' returned no matches.
docs/dualvm/dualvm_submission_final.md includes 'PVM Integration (Live, Not Decorative)' and states the product-path LendingCore uses PvmQuoteProbe at 0x9a78F65b00E0AeD0830063eD0ea66a0B5d8876DE.

docs/dualvm/dualvm_vm_interop_proof.md states the exact capability boundary was proven on the live public testnet; its historical before/after section is explicitly framed as prior-state context rather than a current contradictory claim.

frictions: An initial broad grep across the full repo was truncated, so the audit switched to narrower README/SPEC/features/STATUS/docs/dualvm searches for attributable evidence.
